ucs.title: [LLM] Star Blizzard (SEABORGIUM/COLDRIVER) AiTM session-cookie replay → mailbox forwarding rule
ucs.description: Hunts the canonical SEABORGIUM/COLDRIVER credential-theft chain: a recipient clicks an inbound phishing link routed through an EvilGinx-class AiTM proxy, the stolen session cookie is then replayed from a different IP/geo without an interactive MFA challenge (T1539 → T1550.004), and the actor immediately drops a mailbox forwarding/redirect rule on the compromised mailbox to siphon mail (T1114.003). Microsoft (Star Blizzard, Aug 2022), CISA (Dec 2023) and the UK NCSC explicitly call out this exact mail-exfil pattern — it is THE signature Star Blizzard chain.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) AS signin_time, values(Authentication.src) AS replay_ip, values(Authentication.app) AS app FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.action=success Authentication.signature_id IN ("UserLoggedIn","UserLoggedInFromNewCountry") Authentication.app="*Browser*" BY Authentication.user, Authentication.session_id
| rename Authentication.user AS user
| join type=inner user [
    search (sourcetype=o365:management:activity OR sourcetype=ms:o365:management) (Operation="New-InboxRule" OR Operation="Set-InboxRule" OR Operation="UpdateInboxRules" OR Operation="Set-Mailbox")
    | spath input=Parameters output=ParamRaw
    | search ParamRaw="*ForwardTo*" OR ParamRaw="*RedirectTo*" OR ParamRaw="*ForwardingSmtpAddress*" OR ParamRaw="*DeleteMessage*" OR ParamRaw="*MoveToFolder*RSS*"
    | rename UserId AS user
    | eval rule_time=_time, rule_ip=ClientIP, rule_op=Operation, rule_params=ParamRaw
    | table user rule_time rule_ip rule_op rule_params
]
| eval delta_min=round((rule_time-signin_time)/60,1)
| where delta_min>=0 AND delta_min<=1440
| join type=left user [
    | tstats summariesonly=t count AS clicks, values(All_Email.url) AS clicked_url, min(_time) AS click_time FROM datamodel=Email WHERE All_Email.action="clicked" BY All_Email.recipient
    | rename All_Email.recipient AS user
]
| eval click_to_signin_min=round((signin_time-click_time)/60,1)
| where isnull(click_time) OR click_to_signin_min<=60
| table user click_time clicked_url signin_time replay_ip app rule_time rule_ip rule_op rule_params delta_min click_to_signin_min
| sort - rule_time
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let ReplayWindowMin = 60;     // cookie replay must follow click closely
let RuleWindowHours = 24h;    // mailbox rule typically lands same-day
// 1. Inbound URL click — Star Blizzard routes lures through Mailgun/Proton/Resend redirects to EvilGinx-class AiTM landings.
let SuspectClicks = UrlClickEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActionType in ("ClickAllowed","ClickedThrough")
    | project ClickTime = Timestamp, ClickUpn = AccountUpn, ClickIP = IPAddress, Url, NetworkMessageId;
// 2. Successful AAD browser sign-in for the SAME user from a DIFFERENT IP within 60min, where MFA was non-interactive (cookie/PRT replay — T1550.004).
let TokenReplay = AADSignInEventsBeta
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ErrorCode == 0
    | where ClientAppUsed == "Browser"
    | join kind=inner SuspectClicks on $left.AccountUpn == $right.ClickUpn
    | where Timestamp between (ClickTime .. ClickTime + ReplayWindowMin * 1m)
    | where IPAddress != ClickIP                                  // replay from elsewhere, not the legit user
    | extend MfaUsed = tostring(parse_json(MfaDetail).AuthMethod)
    | where isempty(MfaUsed) or AuthenticationRequirement == "singleFactorAuthentication"
    | project SigninTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, AccountObjectId,
              ReplayIP = IPAddress, ReplayCountry = Country, Application,
              ClickTime, ClickIP, Url;
// 3. New mailbox forwarding/redirect rule on that mailbox within 24h — SEABORGIUM's canonical mail-exfil persistence (T1114.003).
CloudAppEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType in~ ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Set-Mailbox","Add-MailboxPermission")
| extend RawParams = tostring(RawEventData)
| where RawParams has_any ("ForwardTo","RedirectTo","ForwardingSmtpAddress","DeleteMessage","MoveToFolder")
| join kind=inner TokenReplay on $left.AccountObjectId == $right.AccountObjectId
| where Timestamp between (SigninTime .. SigninTime + RuleWindowHours)
| project RuleTime = Timestamp, AccountUpn, RuleAction = ActionType, RuleSrcIP = IPAddress,
          ReplayIP, ReplayCountry, ClickIP, Url, ClickTime, SigninTime, RawParams
| order by RuleTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let ReplayWindowMin = 60m;
let RuleWindowHours = 24h;
// 1. Successful AAD browser sign-ins where MFA was satisfied non-interactively ("Previously satisfied" / cookie / PRT) — AiTM token replay tell.
let TokenReplay = SigninLogs
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ResultType == 0
    | where ClientAppUsed == "Browser"
    | extend AuthDetails = tostring(AuthenticationDetails)
    | where AuthDetails has "Previously satisfied"
        or AuthenticationRequirement == "singleFactorAuthentication"
    | extend Country = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).countryOrRegion),
             City    = tostring(parse_json(LocationDetails).city)
    | project SigninTime = TimeGenerated, UserPrincipalName, UserId,
              ReplayIP = IPAddress, ReplayCountry = Country, ReplayCity = City, AppDisplayName, AuthDetails;
// 2. Mailbox forwarding/redirect rule created on the same mailbox within 24h — SEABORGIUM mail exfil (T1114.003).
OfficeActivity
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where OfficeWorkload == "Exchange"
| where Operation in~ ("New-InboxRule","Set-InboxRule","UpdateInboxRules","Set-Mailbox","Add-MailboxPermission")
| extend ParamsStr = tostring(Parameters)
| where ParamsStr has_any ("ForwardTo","RedirectTo","ForwardingSmtpAddress","DeleteMessage","MoveToFolder")
| join kind=inner TokenReplay on $left.UserId == $right.UserPrincipalName
| where TimeGenerated between (SigninTime .. SigninTime + RuleWindowHours)
| extend MinutesSinceSignin = datetime_diff('minute', TimeGenerated, SigninTime)
| project RuleTime = TimeGenerated, UserId, RuleSrcIP = ClientIP, Operation, ParamsStr,
          SigninTime, ReplayIP, ReplayCountry, ReplayCity, AppDisplayName, MinutesSinceSignin
| order by RuleTime desc
ucs.rationale: Star Blizzard's Microsoft / CISA / NCSC profile is dominated by precision spearphishing of NATO academia/think-tanks/NGOs that ends in browser-based AiTM credential harvest and mailbox surveillance — they explicitly do NOT drop malware in the typical case. The triple-correlation (click → cookie replay from foreign IP without interactive MFA → forwarding rule on the same mailbox within 24h) is stru

_field_issues.message: `MfaDetail` is not a column of AADSignInEventsBeta, CloudAppEvents, UrlClickEvents; it does exist on SigninLogs.

ucs.title: [LLM] Star Blizzard SPICA loader — browser/PDF reader spawning user-staged executable with rare-destination C2
ucs.description: Hunts the COLDRIVER SPICA delivery chain disclosed by Google TAG (Jan 2024): targets receive a benign-looking encrypted-PDF lure, are then directed to download a 'decryption tool' (the SPICA Rust backdoor) which is executed from %USERPROFILE%\Downloads or %TEMP% and immediately beacons to attacker infrastructure. Detects the parent→child→net handshake within 10 minutes, scoped to user-staging directories and lure-aligned filenames.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) AS spawn_time, values(Processes.process) AS cmd, values(Processes.process_hash) AS sha256, values(Processes.parent_process_name) AS parent FROM datamodel=Endpoint.Processes WHERE Processes.parent_process_name IN ("msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","outlook.exe","acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","winword.exe") AND (Processes.process_path="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Temp\\*") AND (Processes.process_name="*.exe" OR Processes.process_name="*.scr" OR Processes.process_name="*.hta" OR Processes.process_name="*.lnk") BY Processes.dest, Processes.user, Processes.process_name
| rename Processes.* AS *
| search (process_name="*decrypt*" OR process_name="*decode*" OR process_name="*proton*" OR process_name="*secure*" OR process_name="*reader*" OR process_name="*viewer*" OR process_name="*verify*" OR parent IN ("acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe"))
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=t min(_time) AS net_time, values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) AS remote_ip, values(All_Traffic.dest_port) AS remote_port FROM datamodel=Network_Traffic WHERE All_Traffic.dest_category!="internal" BY All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.app
    | rename All_Traffic.src AS dest All_Traffic.app AS net_app
]
| eval delta_sec=net_time-spawn_time
| where delta_sec>=0 AND delta_sec<=600
| table spawn_time dest user parent process_name sha256 remote_ip remote_port net_time delta_sec
| sort - spawn_time
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let StagingDirs = dynamic([@"\Downloads\", @"\Temp\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\"]);
let LureKeywords = dynamic(["decrypt","decode","secure","protect","proton","reader","viewer","verify","document"]);
let PdfReaders = dynamic(["acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","foxitreader.exe","sumatrapdf.exe"]);
let UserSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","outlook.exe","acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","winword.exe","foxitreader.exe")
    | where FolderPath has_any (StagingDirs)
    | where FileName endswith ".exe" or FileName endswith ".scr" or FileName endswith ".hta" or FileName endswith ".lnk"
    | where tolower(FileName) has_any (LureKeywords)
        or InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (PdfReaders)
        or InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "outlook.exe"
    | project SpawnTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              Loader = FileName, LoaderPath = FolderPath, LoaderHash = SHA256,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine, ChildPid = ProcessId;
// Pair with same-host outbound to public destination within 10 minutes — SPICA C2 handshake.
UserSpawn
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","outlook.exe","acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","svchost.exe")
    | project NetTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort,
              NetParent = InitiatingProcessFileName, NetCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine
  ) on DeviceId
| where NetTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + 10m)
| where NetParent =~ Loader or NetCmd has tostring(Loader)
| project SpawnTime, NetTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, SpawnTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, Parent, Loader, LoaderPath, LoaderHash,
          RemoteIP, RemoteUrl, RemotePort, ChildCmd
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let PdfReaders = dynamic(["acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","foxitreader.exe","sumatrapdf.exe"]);
let UserSpawn = ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any ("msedge.exe","chrome.exe","firefox.exe","outlook.exe","acrord32.exe","acrobat.exe","winword.exe","foxitreader.exe")
    | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (@"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\AppData\Roaming\", @"\Temp\")
    | where TargetProcessName endswith ".exe" or TargetProcessName endswith ".scr" or TargetProcessName endswith ".hta"
    | where tolower(TargetProcessName) has_any ("decrypt","decode","secure","protect","proton","reader","viewer","verify","document")
        or ParentProcessName has_any (PdfReaders)
        or ParentProcessName has "outlook.exe"
    | project SpawnTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              Parent = ParentProcessName, ParentCmd = ParentProcessCommandLine,
              Loader = TargetProcessName, LoaderCmd = TargetProcessCommandLine, LoaderHash = SHA256;
UserSpawn
| join kind=inner (
    ImNetworkSession
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where not(ipv4_is_private(DstIpAddr))
    | project NetTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url
  ) on DvcHostname
| where NetTime between (SpawnTime .. SpawnTime + 10m)
| extend DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', NetTime, SpawnTime)
| project SpawnTime, NetTime, DelaySec, DvcHostname, ActorUsername, Parent, Loader, LoaderHash,
          DstIpAddr, DstPortNumber, Url, LoaderCmd
| order by SpawnTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Star Blizzard SPICA Loader Spawn From Browser/PDF Reader
id: 7c8e2b94-5a91-4b7f-a3c2-coldriverspica
status: experimental
description: Detects an executable spawned by a browser, mail client, Word, or PDF reader from a user-staging directory with SPICA-aligned lure filenames — aligned with Google TAG's Jan 2024 COLDRIVER report on the SPICA Rust backdoor.
references:
  - https://blog.google/threat-analysis-group/google-tag-coldriver-russian-phishing-malware/
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1033/
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.initial-access
  - attack.execution
  - attack.t1204.002
  - attack.t1059.007
  - attack.g1033
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\msedge.exe'
      - '\chrome.exe'
      - '\firefox.exe'
      - '\outlook.exe'
      - '\acrord32.exe'
      - '\acrobat.exe'
      - '\winword.exe'
      - '\foxitreader.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\Downloads\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\Temp\'
  selection_ext:
    Image|endswith:
      - '.exe'
      - '.scr'
      - '.hta'
  selection_lure:
    Image|contains:
      - 'decrypt'
      - 'decode'
      - 'secure'
      - 'protect'
      - 'proton'
      - 'reader'
      - 'viewer'
      - 'verify'
      - 'document'
  filter_signed_legit:
    Signed: 'true'
    Company:
      - 'Microsoft Corporation'
      - 'Adobe Inc.'
      - 'Google LLC'
      - 'Mozilla Corporation'
  condition: selection_parent and selection_path and selection_ext and selection_lure and not filter_signed_legit
fields:
  - User
  - Image
  - ParentImage
  - CommandLine
  - Hashes
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate user-installed software downloaded via browser (Zoom/Teams/Slack updaters)
  - Adobe / Foxit auxiliary updaters
level: high
ucs.rationale: Star Blizzard is overwhelmingly a credential-phishing actor, but Google TAG's January 2024 disclosure and follow-up CISA advisories tied COLDRIVER to a custom Rust backdoor (SPICA) delivered as a fake 'PDF decryptor' — a pattern not seen in their earlier campaigns and not in commodity stealer playbooks. The detection narrows on the exact tradecraft: a PDF/browser parent spawning an executable from
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID
